leadText: The Premier Ball (Japanese: プレミアボール Premier Ball) is a type of Poké Ball introduced in Generation III. It can be used to catch a wild Pokémon. It was developed by the Devon Corporation.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag in a wild encounter, it attempts to catch the wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1×. If used on an Ultra Beast , the catch rate modifier is instead always set to 410/4096× (~0.1×). The Premier Ball cannot be used in situations in which Poké Balls cannot be used, such as in wild battles with two or more opponents currently present or against a trial Pokémon. If used in a Trainer battle (except if used as a Snag Ball on a Shadow Pokémon ), the opposing Trainer will deflect it, wasting the player's turn (as well as the ball prior to Generation IV ). If used on the ghost Marowak , it will dodge it, wasting the ball. Held item [ edit | edit source ] Fling fails if the user is holding a Premier Ball.

Destroy Floating Poké Ball in Hyperspace Zones with Item Power: Poké Balls Distribution [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period Sw Sh Pokémon Direct Premier Balls All January 9 to February 27, 2020 S V Japanese 7-11 Onigiri Balls Japan November 18, 2022 to January 10, 2023 NPC usage [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon [ edit | edit source ] Collectors keep their Pokémon in Premier Balls. Pokémon Scarlet and Violet [ edit | edit source ] Cyrano keeps his Pokémon in Premier Balls. In addition, Clavell is seen carrying six Premier Balls, but the Pokémon he uses in battle are kept in standard Poké Balls. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Naveen keeps his Pokémon in Premier Balls.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] A Dynamaxed Poké Ball in Pokémon Journeys: The Series The Premier Ball is the only Poké Ball type not introduced in Generation I that is usable by the player in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . When a Poké Ball is Dynamaxed , it resembles a giant Premier Ball.
